reports-alv-010.quickRevisionNotes:
What: ALV layout variants for column order, width and visibility settings.

Used for:
- Persisting user-specific or global layouts across sessions
- Applying a default variant on first display
- Driving variant choice from a selection-screen parameter

Key syntax / tables / Tcodes:
- DISVARIANT structure with report, handle, variant and save
- save = 'A' all, 'U' user only, 'X' global only
- i_default and i_save on set_table_for_first_display
- Default variant convention /DEFAULT
- Variants persisted in table LTDX

Interview keyword: DISVARIANT plus save = 'A' plus /DEFAULT variant

followupAnswerBank.question: What is the LTDX table and what does it store?
followupAnswerBank.hinglishExplanation: LTDX wo SAP database table hai jahan ALV layout variants persist hote hain. Key fields mein RELID shamil hai jo object type identify karta hai, program name, ALV handle name, variant name aur serialized layout data. Variant issues troubleshoot karte waqt, main SM30 use karta hoon LTDX browse karne ke liye — program name aur handle se filter karke — stale ya conflicting variant entries identify karne ke liye jinhe delete karna hai.
followupAnswerBank.interviewAnswer: LTDX is the SAP database table where ALV layout variants are persisted. Key fields include RELID identifying the object type, the program name, ALV handle name, variant name, and the serialized layout data. When troubleshooting variant issues, I use SM30 to browse LTDX — filtering by program name and handle — to identify stale or conflicting variant entries that need to be deleted.
followupAnswerBank.realtimeExplanation: Ticket: 'ALV layout error after program change.' → SM30 → LTDX → filter by report name → find variant → check if field references match current fieldcat → delete if stale → user recreates variant.
followupAnswerBank.mistakes: Trying to manually edit LTDX data — it stores binary serialized data, direct editing will corrupt the variant. Only delete entries from LTDX, never try to modify the layout data directly.
followupAnswerBank.codeExample:
" Delete stale variant from LTDX programmatically (use with caution)
DATA: ls_variant TYPE disvariant.
ls_variant-report  = 'ZOLD_REPORT'.
ls_variant-handle  = 'MAIN'.
ls_variant-variant = 'STALE_VAR'.
CALL FUNCTION 'LT_VARIANT_DELETE'
  EXPORTING
    is_variant = ls_variant
  EXCEPTIONS
    OTHERS     = 1.

followupAnswerBank.question: What is the difference between user-specific and global variants?
followupAnswerBank.hinglishExplanation: User-specific variants private hote hain — sirf banane wale user ko visible, LTDX mein unke user ID ke saath store hote hain. Global variants saare users ke across shared hote hain aur typically standard business view represent karte hain jo admin ya functional team configure karta hai. ABAP mein, main isse is_variant-save use karke control karta hoon: 'U' sirf user-specific saves allow karta hai, 'X' sirf global saves allow karta hai, 'A' dono allow karta hai. Global variants transported hote hain; user-specific nahi.
followupAnswerBank.interviewAnswer: User-specific variants are private — only visible to the user who created them, stored with their user ID in LTDX. Global variants are shared across all users and typically represent the standard business view configured by an admin or functional team. In ABAP, I control this using is_variant-save: 'U' allows only user-specific saves, 'X' allows only global saves, 'A' allows both. Global variants are transported; user-specific ones are not.
followupAnswerBank.realtimeExplanation: Go-live: functional team creates global variant '/STANDARD' with agreed-upon column layout. Users can additionally save their own user-specific preferences. This separation keeps the standard view stable.
followupAnswerBank.mistakes: Allowing all users to save global variants — any user can overwrite the standard layout. Restrict global variant save to admin role using authorization object S_ALV_LAYO.
followupAnswerBank.codeExample:
" Restricting who can save global variants
" Check authorization before allowing global save
AUTHORITY-CHECK OBJECT 'S_ALV_LAYO'
  ID 'ACTVT' FIELD '02'.  " 02 = change/save
IF sy-subrc <> 0.
  is_variant-save = 'U'.  " Only user-specific save allowed
ELSE.
  is_variant-save = 'A'.  " Admin: allow global save too
ENDIF.

followupAnswerBank.question: How do you set a default variant in ALV?
followupAnswerBank.hinglishExplanation: Ek default variant automatically apply hota hai jab ALV explicit user selection ke bina load hoti hai. SAP ka convention hai default variant ko '/DEFAULT' name karna. Code mein, main '/DEFAULT' ko set_table_for_first_display ke i_default parameter mein pass karta hoon. Agar yeh variant LTDX mein exist karta hai, yeh automatically load hota hai; nahi toh, ALV standard field catalog layout ke saath load hoti hai. Default variant typically UAT ke dauran functional team banata hai aur program ke saath transported hota hai.
followupAnswerBank.interviewAnswer: A default variant is automatically applied when the ALV loads without explicit user selection. SAP's convention is to name the default variant '/DEFAULT'. In code, I pass '/DEFAULT' in the i_default parameter of set_table_for_first_display. If this variant exists in LTDX, it loads automatically; if not, the ALV loads with the standard field catalog layout. The default variant is typically created by the functional team during UAT and transported with the program.
followupAnswerBank.realtimeExplanation: Requirement: 'ALV should always open with first 5 columns visible, sorted by date.' → Functional team sets up layout → saves as global variant '/DEFAULT' → tested in QA → transported to production.
followupAnswerBank.mistakes: Hard-coding a variant name other than '/DEFAULT' as the default — if the variant doesn't exist in the target system, ALV loads without any layout which can confuse users.
followupAnswerBank.codeExample:
" Apply default variant automatically on load
CALL METHOD go_alv->set_table_for_first_display
  EXPORTING
    is_variant  = is_variant     " report + handle filled
    i_default   = '/DEFAULT'     " SAP standard default variant name
    i_save      = 'A'

followupAnswerBank.question: What happens when a variant has fields not in the current field catalog?
followupAnswerBank.hinglishExplanation: Jab ek saved variant kisi field ko reference karta hai jo ab field catalog mein exist nahi karta — program change ki wajah se — variant invalid ho jaata hai. Behavior ALV version pe depend karta hai: yeh error dikha sakta hai, missing field ko silently ignore kar sakta hai, ya default layout pe fall back ho sakta hai. Fix hai LTDX se ya ALV ke Manage Layout option se stale variant delete karna, aur updated field catalog ke based pe usse recreate karna. Yeh scenario production mein program enhancements ke baad common hai.
followupAnswerBank.interviewAnswer: When a saved variant references a field that no longer exists in the field catalog — due to a program change — the variant becomes invalid. Behavior depends on ALV version: it may show an error, silently ignore the missing field, or fall back to the default layout. The fix is to delete the stale variant from LTDX or through ALV's Manage Layout option, and recreate it based on the updated field catalog. This scenario is common after program enhancements in production.
followupAnswerBank.realtimeExplanation: Post-transport issue: 'After today's transport, ALV is not loading correctly.' Root cause: field removed from fieldcat, old variant still references it. Fix: delete variant in production via ALV UI or LTDX, recreate and re-transport if needed.
followupAnswerBank.mistakes: Keeping old variants without cleanup after field catalog changes. Always communicate to users that existing variants may need to be recreated after significant program changes.
followupAnswerBank.codeExample:
" Safe approach: catch variant load errors
TRY.
  CALL METHOD go_alv->set_table_for_first_display
    EXPORTING
      is_variant = is_variant.
CATCH cx_salv_msg INTO DATA(lo_ex).
  MESSAGE lo_ex->get_text( ) TYPE 'W'.
  " Fall back: load without variant
  CLEAR is_variant-variant.
  CALL METHOD go_alv->set_table_for_first_display
    EXPORTING is_variant = is_variant.
ENDTRY.

followupAnswerBank.question: How do you troubleshoot a variant that is not loading correctly?
followupAnswerBank.hinglishExplanation: Troubleshooting steps: pehle verify karo ki ABAP code mein variant name, report name aur handle exactly wahi hain jo LTDX mein hain. Phir LTDX pe SM30 check karo confirm karne ke liye ki variant entry exist karti hai. Agar variant exist karta hai lekin incorrectly load hota hai, ALV ke Change Layout — Manage use karo problematic variants inspect aur delete karne ke liye. Mismatches identify karne ke liye variant ke column references ko current field catalog ke against compare karo. Agar issue transport-related hai, verify karo ki latest program change ke baad variant transported hua tha.
followupAnswerBank.interviewAnswer: Troubleshooting steps: first verify the variant name, report name, and handle in the ABAP code match exactly what's in LTDX. Then check SM30 on LTDX to confirm the variant entry exists. If the variant exists but loads incorrectly, use ALV's Change Layout — Manage to inspect and delete problematic variants. Compare the variant's column references against the current field catalog to identify mismatches. If the issue is transport-related, verify the variant was transported after the latest program change.
followupAnswerBank.realtimeExplanation: Troubleshoot checklist: code correct? → LTDX entry exists? → fieldcat match? → correct system? → transport sequence correct? Go through each systematically rather than guessing.
followupAnswerBank.mistakes: Assuming variant issue is always a code bug. Half the time it is a configuration issue — variant not transported, wrong handle name, or user saved a personal variant that overrides the global one.
followupAnswerBank.codeExample:
" Debug: print variant being used
WRITE: / 'Variant report:', is_variant-report.
WRITE: / 'Variant handle:', is_variant-handle.
WRITE: / 'Variant name:', is_variant-variant.
" Then check SM30 LTDX with these exact values to confirm entry exists

followupAnswerBank.question: What is the HANDLE parameter in ALV variant configuration?
followupAnswerBank.hinglishExplanation: HANDLE parameter ek program ke andar har ALV instance ko uniquely identify karta hai, jo tab important hota hai jab ek single program ke screen pe multiple ALV grids hote hain. Yeh LTDX mein key ka part use hota hai har ALV ke liye variants separately store aur retrieve karne ke liye. Agar do ALVs same handle share karte hain, unke variants collide ho jaate hain. Best practice: meaningful handle names use karo jaise 'HEADER_ALV' aur 'ITEM_ALV' variant storage separate aur LTDX mein clearly identifiable rakhne ke liye.
followupAnswerBank.interviewAnswer: The HANDLE parameter uniquely identifies each ALV instance within a program, which is important when a single program has multiple ALV grids on screen. It is used as part of the key in LTDX to store and retrieve variants separately for each ALV. If two ALVs share the same handle, their variants collide. Best practice: use meaningful handle names like 'HEADER_ALV' and 'ITEM_ALV' to keep variant storage separate and clearly identifiable in LTDX.
followupAnswerBank.realtimeExplanation: Screen with two ALVs: header data on top, line items below. Handle for first: 'HDR'. Handle for second: 'ITM'. LTDX stores two separate variant sets — no collision, each ALV remembers its own layout independently.
followupAnswerBank.mistakes: Using the same handle or leaving it blank for multiple ALVs on one screen — variants overwrite each other and users get wrong layouts on one or both grids.
followupAnswerBank.codeExample:
" Two ALVs on same screen — different handles
DATA: is_var_hdr TYPE disvariant,
      is_var_itm TYPE disvariant.
is_var_hdr-report = sy-repid.
is_var_hdr-handle = 'HDR_ALV'.   " Unique handle for header ALV
is_var_itm-report = sy-repid.
is_var_itm-handle = 'ITM_ALV'.   " Unique handle for item ALV
" LTDX stores separately: ZREPORT/HDR_ALV and ZREPORT/ITM_ALV

followupAnswerBank.question: How are ALV variants transported between systems?
followupAnswerBank.hinglishExplanation: ALV variants ek Customizing transport request mein LTDX ki table entries ke roop mein transport hote hain — Workbench request nahi. Critical best practice yeh hai ki variant ko program transport hone aur target system mein verify hone ke BAAD transport karo. Variant ko program se pehle transport karne se mismatch ho sakta hai agar fieldcat change ho gaya ho. Main task description mein transport sequence document karta hoon ensure karne ke liye ki BASIS unhe correct order mein import kare.
followupAnswerBank.interviewAnswer: ALV variants are transported as table entries from LTDX in a Customizing transport request — not a Workbench request. The critical best practice is to transport the variant AFTER the program has been transported and verified in the target system. Transporting the variant before the program can result in a mismatch if the fieldcat changed. I document the transport sequence in the task description to ensure BASIS imports them in the correct order.
followupAnswerBank.realtimeExplanation: Transport sequence mistake: variant TR imported before program TR in production → variant references old fieldcat → ALV error next day. Lesson: always specify transport sequence in release notes.
followupAnswerBank.mistakes: Not specifying transport order — BASIS may import variant before program, causing mismatch. Always write explicit import sequence instructions: 'Program TR first, then Variant TR.'
followupAnswerBank.codeExample:
" Variant transport: add LTDX entries to Customizing TR
" In SE10: New request → Customizing → Add table entries
" Table: LTDX
" Key: RELID='LT', SRTF2='ZPROGRAM', HANDLE='MAIN', VARIANT='ZDEFAULT'
" Release variant TR only AFTER program TR is confirmed in target

followupAnswerBank.question: What are best practices for ALV variant management in projects?
followupAnswerBank.hinglishExplanation: Best practices: meaningful, business-oriented variant names use karo jaise '/FINANCE' ya '/STANDARD_VIEW'. Sirf program stable hone ke baad variants transport karo fieldcat mismatches avoid karne ke liye. Global variant creation ko S_ALV_LAYO use karke authorized roles tak restrict karo. Expected variants ko technical spec mein document karo aur post-transport production mein validate karo. End users ko variant management pe train karo — personal variants kaise create, change aur delete karein — support tickets reduce karne ke liye.
followupAnswerBank.interviewAnswer: Best practices: use meaningful, business-oriented variant names like '/FINANCE' or '/STANDARD_VIEW'. Only transport variants after the program is stable to avoid fieldcat mismatches. Restrict global variant creation to authorized roles using S_ALV_LAYO. Document expected variants in the technical spec and validate them in production post-transport. Train end users on variant management — how to create, change, and delete personal variants — to reduce support tickets.
followupAnswerBank.realtimeExplanation: Project go-live checklist for variants: meaningful names defined? → global variants created in QA? → authorization tested? → transported after program? → validated in production? → users trained? All 6 checked = smooth go-live.
followupAnswerBank.mistakes: Not documenting variants — after go-live when support team handles tickets they don't know which variants are expected, leading to confusion and wrong deletions.
followupAnswerBank.codeExample:
" Variant naming convention in code
is_variant-variant = '/STANDARD'.    " Global default for all users
" User-specific: created interactively by users
" Convention: global variants start with '/' (SAP standard practice)
" is_variant-save = 'A' allows both global and user variants
" is_variant-save = 'U' allows only user-specific variants
